statements.impact_statement: This image uses wolfSSL FIPS v5.2.3 (Certificate #4718) as the cryptographic backend via wolfCrypt JNI (JCE provider) and wolfSSL JNI (JSSE provider). OpenSSL is not used for cryptographic operations. All crypto operations are performed by wolfSSL FIPS-validated module.

OpenJDK 19 JDK (Debian Bookworm) with custom java.security policy. wolfSSL providers configured as security.provider.1 and security.provider.2. crypto.policy=unlimited, keystore.type=WKS. MD5, SHA-1, DSA, RC4, DES and weak algorithms disabled via jdk.tls.disabledAlgorithms, jdk.certpath.disabledAlgorithms, and jdk.jar.disabledAlgorithms.

statements.action_statement: Monitoring wolfSSL security advisories. FIPS 140-3 Certificate #4718 validation ensures cryptographic module integrity. wolfSSL built with --enable-fips=v5 --enable-jni --disable-md5. FIPS in-core integrity hash verified via fips-hash.sh during build.

statements.impact_statement: STRICT FIPS POLICY: MD5, MD4, MD2, and SHA-1 disabled via java.security policy (jdk.tls.disabledAlgorithms, jdk.certpath.disabledAlgorithms, jdk.jar.disabledAlgorithms). wolfSSL built with --disable-md5 and FIPS v5 mode. wolfCrypt JNI and wolfSSL JNI provide only FIPS-approved algorithms. Only SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, AES, RSA (≥2048), and ECDSA available.
Log4j library not included in this image. Only OpenJDK 19 JDK runtime with wolfSSL FIPS providers is present. No application logging frameworks included.
